hero.desc: OCPP charge point management platform, designed for charging operators, enterprise users and developers. Supports OCPP 1.6 standard protocol, compatible with mainstream charger brands.
hero.desc2: Helping new and investment-oriented charging operators quickly build and manage charging stations. With a lightweight platform as the core, leverage a complete remote management, settlement and operation system to easily run a profitable business.

businessFlow.label: Platform Architecture
businessFlow.title: How GCSS Connects Everything
businessFlow.desc: An interactive overview of how drivers, chargers, operators, and the GCSS platform work together
businessFlow.descUser: Initiates charging session via QR scan. Receives status updates from server.
businessFlow.descQr: QR code scanned by the user to identify and connect to the charger.
businessFlow.descCharger: Physical EV charging unit. Communicates power delivery data to the server.
businessFlow.descServer: Central orchestration engine. Manages authentication, sessions, and data routing.
businessFlow.descCpo: Charge Point Operator. Manages infrastructure and receives revenue flows.
businessFlow.descAdmin: Super Admin layer. Oversees multiple CPOs and global system health.

models.label: Business Models
models.title: Flexible Operating Models for Every Scale
models.desc: Whether you're an independent operator or building a platform, GCSS adapts to your business model.

b2c.title: Independent Operator (B2C)
b2c.desc: Perfect for new operators and investors. Manage your own charging stations with remote monitoring, automated billing, and smart computing.
flow: 1. Transaction Initiation: The Driver interacts with the EV Charger by scanning a QR code.
2. Money Flow In: The Driver pays via card or mobile payment. This payment goes directly to the central Server.
3. Data Flow In: Simultaneously, the EV Charger sends real-time operational data (charging status, session details, etc.) to the central Server.

4. Money & Data to Operator: The Server processes both streams. It routes the transaction funds directly to the single CPO (Charge Point Operator) and sends operational data to that same CPO, allowing them to monitor the hardware and confirm the payment.
b2c.summary: It is a direct, linear relationship. The CPO owns the charger, receives 100% of the data, and collects 100% of the processed revenue directly from the end-user.

b2b.title: SaaS Platform Edition (B2B2C)
b2b.desc: For enterprises building large-scale charging networks. Multi-tenant architecture supporting unlimited operators with a unified Super Admin console.
flow: 1. Transaction Initiation: Similar to B2C, a Driver interacts with an EV Charger via a QR code.
2. Money Flow In: The Driver makes a payment, which goes to the central Server.
3. Data Flow In: The EV Charger sends its operational data to the central Server.

4. Distribution to Sub-Operators: The central Server identifies which specific charger was used. It then distributes the transaction funds and the localized operational data to the specific CPO managing that charger.

5. Data Aggregation: All individual CPOs feed their operational data up to a central dashboard managed by the CPO Super Administrator. This gives the B2B client a complete, aggregated view of the entire network's performance.
6. Revenue Sharing: Along with the data, money also flows upward. The individual CPOs send a percentage of their earnings to the CPO Super Administrator.
b2b.summary: It is a hierarchical relationship. Your B2B client (the Super Admin) acts as the platform provider. They provide the network infrastructure to multiple independent CPOs. In return, the Super Admin receives aggregated, system-wide data and collects a commission/percentage of the revenue generated by all the sub-operators below them.

install.title: Installation Guide

step1.title: Step 1: Prepare a Linux Server
step1.desc: GCSS supports deployment on mainstream Linux distributions including CentOS, Ubuntu, and Debian. The following CPU architectures are supported:
step1.req: Ensure your server has at least 2 CPU cores, 4GB RAM, and 40GB disk space for a smooth installation. A clean OS installation is recommended.

step2.title: Step 2: Run Installation Script
step2.desc: Connect to your server via SSH and run the following command to start the automated installation:
step2.note: The script will automatically detect your system environment, install required dependencies, and configure the GCSS platform. The entire process typically takes 5-10 minutes depending on your server specs and network speed.
step2.warning: Make sure ports 80, 443, and 8080 are open in your firewall before running the installation.

step3.title: Step 3: Access Admin Panel
step3.desc: Once the installation completes, the script will display the access URL for your admin panel. Open your browser and navigate to the provided URL (typically http://your-server-ip:port).
step3.note: Log in with the default administrator credentials shown at the end of the installation output. We strongly recommend changing the default password immediately after your first login.

install.intro: Follow these steps to install GCSS on your server.

visual.title: Empowering EV Management for the Future

hero.title: Build Your Own EV Charging SaaS Platform
hero.desc: The ultimate multi-tenant solution, supporting unlimited operator onboarding, automated hierarchical management, all controlled through a unified Super Admin console to effortlessly manage your global charging network.

modes.title: Diversified Operation Modes for Ultimate Flexibility
modes.desc: Multiple ready-made platform operation modes to perfectly meet your diverse operational needs.

mode1.title: Unified Network Aggregation
mode1.desc: Seamlessly integrate third-party partners into your proprietary network, aggregate industry resources, and unify O&M management under your partnership framework.

mode2.title: Self-Operated Model
mode2.desc: Build and manage your own charging stations with complete control over membership, product pricing, transaction processing, and financial settlement — all centralized in your management system.

mode3.title: SaaS Franchise Model
mode3.desc: Through franchise agreements, invite partners to join your platform with revenue sharing, SaaS fee collection, and tiered management, leveraging existing resources for rapid expansion.

mode4.title: Hybrid Operation Mode
mode4.desc: Combine the advantages of self-operation, franchising, and SaaS billing. Enable third-party operators to run their business on your platform for maximum market coverage.

revenue.title: Diversified Revenue Channels for Maximum Profitability
revenue.desc: Unlock multiple monetization paths through diversified channels to achieve sustainable income and maximize your platform's overall ROI.

item1.title: Operator Commission & SaaS Fees
item1.desc: Operators joining your network ensure continuous platform revenue. Through customizable commission rates and tiered SaaS billing models, create a stable and scalable income stream.

item2.title: Fund Settlement & Cash Flow
item2.desc: All payment transactions generate fund deposits within the platform, creating cash flow income. Leverage settlement cycles to maximize financial efficiency.

item3.title: Self-Operated Charging Revenue
item3.desc: Run charging stations directly on the platform with 100% direct sales revenue from every transaction.

item4.title: Advertising & Promotion Revenue
item4.desc: Offer advertising placements across App, H5, and push notification channels. Operators can purchase ad spots for targeted marketing campaigns.

feat1.title: Cross-Platform Access
feat1.desc: Support responsive web (H5), native mobile apps, and flexible architecture for seamless third-party integration.

feat2.title: Flexible Deployment
feat2.desc: Choose between GCSS hosted deployment, private on-premise servers, or full cloud SaaS hosting based on your enterprise needs.

feat3.title: Powerful APIs & High Scalability
feat3.desc: Complete set of open APIs for seamless third-party integration, with deep secondary development support from our engineering team.

advantages.title: Exquisite UI/UX, Extraordinary Experience
advantages.desc: Modern, intuitive interfaces with meticulous layout and rigorous interaction design that deeply fits user workflows.
advantages.buildTitle: Build Your Own EV Charging SaaS Platform
advantages.buildDesc: Not just operating charging stations — manage entire charging networks. The ultimate multi-tenant solution for CPOs, fleet managers, and energy providers.
advantages.unlimitedDesc: Support unlimited sub-merchants (hotels, malls, fleet operators, etc.)
advantages.autoBillingDesc: System automatically calculates platform commission and merchant settlement amounts.
advantages.superAdminDesc: Unified control panel for global oversight with real-time statistics.
advantages.payment1: Users pay via App or credit card
advantages.payment2: System automatically deducts platform service fees
advantages.payment3: Remaining balance auto-settled to merchant accounts

profit.title: Diversified Revenue Model
profit.subtitle: Beyond just electricity fees. Our system unlocks 4 streams of passive income.

stream1.title: Automated Platform Fees
stream1.desc: Customize charging commission rates per operator. The system automatically calculates and settles platform fees for each transaction.

stream2.title: Value-Added Services
stream2.desc: Enable "reserved parking" fees, charging time surcharges, and session management charges. Operators can customize their own pricing strategies.

stream3.title: Ad Revenue
stream3.desc: In-App, H5, and push notification ad placements. Operators purchase impressions and targeted campaigns at premium rates.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.desc: Find answers to the most common questions about GCSS EV charging management platform.

items.a1: GCSS (Global Charger System Service) is a comprehensive EV charging management platform that enables operators to manage charging stations, users, payments, and analytics from a single dashboard. It supports both B2C (individual station) and B2B (multi-tenant network) deployment models.
items.q2: Is there a free version available?
items.a2: Yes! GCSS offers a free Community Edition that includes core charging station management features. You can upgrade to Pro or Enterprise plans as your business grows to unlock advanced features like multi-site management, custom branding, and priority support.
items.a3: GCSS supports OCPP 1.6 and OCPP 2.0.1 protocols, which are compatible with the vast majority of EV chargers on the market. We also support integration with chargers via proprietary APIs on request.
items.q4: How do I deploy GCSS on my own server?
items.a4: GCSS can be deployed using Docker containers on any Linux-based server. We provide detailed deployment guides in our documentation, and our support team can assist with the initial setup. The platform requires minimal server resources — 2 CPU cores and 4GB RAM are sufficient for most deployments.
items.a5: GCSS integrates with Stripe, PayPal, Alipay, WeChat Pay, and multiple local payment processors. You can configure multiple payment methods simultaneously and manage all transactions from the unified dashboard.
items.a6: Yes, the Pro and Enterprise plans include full white-labeling capabilities. You can customize the logo, color scheme, domain name, and email templates to match your brand identity. The mobile app can also be published under your own brand.
items.q7: How does billing and revenue sharing work?
items.a7: GCSS handles all billing automatically. You set your charging rates, and the platform manages session billing, payment processing, and revenue reports. For B2B operators, the multi-tenant system supports revenue sharing between network operators and station owners with configurable split ratios.
items.q8: Is there an API for third-party integrations?
items.a8: Yes, GCSS provides a comprehensive RESTful API that allows you to integrate with your existing systems — including fleet management, CRM, ERP, and energy management platforms. Full API documentation with interactive examples is available in our docs.
items.q9: What kind of support do you offer?
items.a9: Community Edition users get access to our forum and documentation. Pro users receive email support with 24-hour response time. Enterprise customers get dedicated support with a named account manager, priority response, and custom SLA agreements.
items.q10: Can I manage multiple locations from one dashboard?
items.a10: Absolutely. GCSS is designed for multi-site management. You can monitor all your charging stations across different locations in real-time, compare performance metrics, and manage operations centrally. The B2B platform extends this further with multi-tenant capabilities.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.desc: Our team is here to help. Reach out and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.

privacyPage.label: Legal
privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.subtitle: How we collect, use, and protect information when you use GCSS.
privacyPage.effective: Effective date: April 20, 2026. This policy is provided for demo purposes and may be updated.

collect.title: Information we collect
collect.p1: We collect information you provide directly and data generated by your use of the platform.
collect.li1: Account and contact details (e.g., name, email, company)
collect.li2: Operational data (e.g., charging sessions, station status, usage metrics)
collect.li3: Technical data (e.g., device, browser, approximate location, logs)

use.title: How we use information
use.p1: We use information to provide, maintain, and improve GCSS.
use.li1: Operate the service and support customers
use.li3: Comply with legal obligations and prevent abuse

security.title: Security
security.p1: We apply industry-standard safeguards, but no system can be guaranteed 100% secure. Please use strong passwords and restrict access to admin accounts.

cookies.title: Cookies & analytics
cookies.p1: We may use cookies or similar technologies to remember preferences and understand how the site is used.

rights.title: Your choices
rights.p1: Depending on your region, you may request access, correction, or deletion of your information.

contact.title: Contact
contact.p1: Questions about privacy? Contact our team and we’ll help.

privacyPage.callout: Enterprise evaluations: we can provide a tailored DPA and security questionnaire response on request.

promoCodes.title: Promo Codes

servers.title: Server Instances

orders.subtitle: View all customer orders, advance their processing + server setup stages, and mark orders paid manually for bank-transfer scenarios.

orders.confirmMarkPaid: Mark this order as paid? This will create an invoice record and should only be used for manual bank transfers.
